steps.title: Identify the contrasting distances
steps.content: JWST is positioned almost one million miles beyond Earth's orbit, while Hubble is only 340 miles above Earth's surface. These vastly different distances create the key relationship to mark.

steps.title: Match the transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two sentences is contrast. JWST sits almost one million miles beyond Earth's orbit; Hubble is only 340 miles above Earth's surface. The blank needs a transition word signaling that opposition.
  • Choice A — ‘By contrast,’: signals direct opposition between two things, so it fits. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Therefore,’: signals cause-effect — the second sentence would be a result of the first. No causal link exists here; the two distances are simply different facts. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Secondly,’: signals sequential addition in an ordered list. No numbered list is set up, so this is the wrong direction. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Similarly,’: signals sameness or likeness. The distances are vastly different, not similar. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
